response:
the engineer models the query response time for each database as a function of the number of queries q it handles  
the total query response time function t_total(q) is the sum of the individual response times of the three databases  
the query distribution is such that mysql handles alpha q, oracle handles beta q, and sybase handles (1 - alpha - beta) q  
the total response time is the sum of the individual response times for each database  
the total response time function is t_total(q) = t_mysql(q) + t_oracle(q) + t_sybase(q)  
the individual response times are: t_mysql(q) = a * q^2 + b * e^(c * q)  
t_oracle(q) = d * ln(q + 1) + e / (q + 1)  
t_sybase(q) = f * q^(3/2) - g * sqrt(q)  
the total response time function is t_total(q) = a * q^2 + b * e^(c * q) + d * ln(q + 1) + e / (q + 1) + f * q^(3/2) - g * sqrt(q)  
the optimal values of alpha and beta are determined by minimizing t_total(q)  
the optimal values of alpha and beta are found by taking the derivative of t_total(q) with respect to alpha and beta  
the derivative of t_total(q) with respect to alpha is the derivative of t_mysql(q) with respect to alpha  
the derivative of t_total(q) with respect to beta is the derivative of t_oracle(q) with respect to beta  
the optimal values of alpha and beta are found by setting the derivatives to zero
